Seraphine.text: Use E proactively to help allies dodge skillshots and extend trades; save W for real all-ins, not light poke.
Move with teammates before objectives; Seraphine is strong setting river vision but weak face-checking fog alone.
Hold W for the first committed dive, then kite backward with shields, speed, and healing.
Locket fits this fight pattern: absorb first contact, then double-W to reset spacing.
Stand behind allies to extend R through the frontline; Seraphine wins by chaining range, not by starting fights alone.

Jinx.text: Use rockets to farm and poke from range; avoid extended trades before support or jungle pressure arrives.
Rotate after bot wave is cleared; Jinx loses tempo when she moves before catching the wave.
Front-to-back only: hit nearest target, preserve Flash, and chase after the first passive reset.
One kill or assist changes the fight; play for reset cleanup instead of forcing first contact.

Caitlyn.text: Use range to control the first three waves; trap under CC or dash landing spots before trading.
Set traps before dragon or tower sieges; Caitlyn controls space before fights, not after they start.
Fight around trap zones and headshot windows; do not walk into melee range for extra autos.
Convert lane priority into plates and objective setup before enemy scaling carries reach two items.

Ezreal.text: Stack passive with Q before trading; missing Q removes most of Ezreal's lane pressure.
Poke before objectives from fog or river angles; Ezreal should lower HP bars before committing.
Hold E until the engage spell is committed; using it forward gives divers a free target.
Win through safe poke, item spikes, and low-death uptime rather than short-range DPS races.

Lux.text: Trade after E slow or support CC; fishing blind Q without wave control gives up pressure.
Control river entrances with E and Q; Lux is strongest before enemies can force melee range.
Hold Q for the first diver instead of using it as poke when engage threats are missing.
Win by chunking carries before objectives, then converting one binding into a pick.

Nautilus.text: Threaten brush hooks at level 2; Nautilus creates pressure by making enemies respect fog.
Roam first when bot wave is pushed; your point-click CC turns river fights into picks.
Ult the carry or key follow-up target, not the nearest tank, unless peeling is mandatory.
Force decisive fights before poke comps can lower your team's HP bars.

Lulu.text: Use W on the enemy carry during all-ins; polymorph often prevents more damage than shielding first.
Stay connected to the fed carry; Lulu loses value when she shadows low-impact side lanes.
Save R and W for the first diver; your job is denying the burst window, not starting the fight.
Keep the primary DPS alive through the first engage, then speed them forward after cooldowns are spent.
